page: Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family. It runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor using the x86_64 architecture. This processor features 24 physical cores and 48 virtual CPUs with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 480 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual CPU.

An architectural tradeoff of this server is the complete absence of local storage, meaning all storage operations must rely on external Premium Storage options. Additionally, the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate vector and matrix operations.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across several categories. The server achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, PassMark CPU scores, and PassMark database operations fall into the strong category. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also strong, though large-block read and write speeds remain in the average tier. The system demonstrates strong capabilities in Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput.

In terms of qualitative cost efficiency, the D48s_v7 offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads, leveraging its dedicated CPU allocation and high clock speed without the added cost of bundled local storage. This configuration is suitable for enterprise database operations, web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and moderate LLM inference tasks using small to medium models.
description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 24 physical cores, 48 vCPUs, and 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The server lacks local storage, requiring external Premium Storage, and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmarks show top-tier capabilities in single-threaded compression and decompression, alongside strong results in PassMark CPU tests, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. This virtual machine is configured for enterprise database operations, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It lacks local storage and delivers strong database and compression performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, designed for database and web workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency processors and advanced matrix extensions for database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
192.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Top-tier single-threaded compression and strong multi-threaded decompression performance
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage configurations
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and AMX for accelerated computing
Suitable for database operations, static web serving, and moderate LLM inference
